response.justification: Bogdan Szabo demonstrates strong full-stack and polyglot development skills with exposure to diverse technologies, but his direct experience with C++ and industrial automation is limited. His background in secure coding, event-driven architectures, and mentorship aligns partially with the role, but the lack of deep C++ expertise and industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443) is a notable gap.
strengths: Extensive experience with event-driven architectures, multi-threading, and distributed systems (e.g., GISCollective, Dunnhumby media).
Proven track record in secure coding practices, mentorship, and technical documentation (e.g., workshops, Refactoring Dojos).
Strong problem-solving and cross-functional collaboration skills, with a focus on testable, high-quality software.
concerns: Limited recent hands-on experience with C++ (last notable C++ work was in 2014–2015 at National Instruments).
No explicit mention of industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443) or Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) practices.
Primary expertise lies in web/mobile (TypeScript, Node.js, React) rather than embedded/industrial automation systems.
